item.thinking: Let me read the check-in protocol file to understand what I need to do.

item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in (PM Cycle) — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job for the **PM build cycle**. Your job is to check whether the afternoon IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The noon job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
PROJECT="$PWD"
cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git" HEAD:gh-pages
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

}

item.thinking:
The PM status file shows:
- `status`: `"waiting_continuation"`
- `total_build_minutes`: 95
- Target: 360 minutes (6 hours)

So this meets condition 5: status is `waiting_continuation` and total_build_minutes (95) < 360. I need to continue the build.

Let me follow the continuation protocol:

1. Read the IWSDK dev skill
2. Read the status file (done - I have context)
3. Update status file to "building"
4. Assess current codebase
5. Plan expansion


6. Build
7. Update status when done
8. Capture journal
9. Update build log and summary

This is a substantial task with real complexity, so I should spawn a subagent to handle the heavy lifting on the actual build work rather than trying to execute it all myself at depth=0.

item.name: subagents
item.arguments:
{"action":"spawn_agent","message":"Continue building Holo Golf VR (round 3 of PM build cycle). The game is at ~/workspace/holo-golf/ with 22 files, 5,156 lines, 27 holes across 3 courses. 95 of 360 target minutes used so far.

**Your task:**

1. Read the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`
2. Assess the current Holo Golf codebase — read source files, check for type errors (`npx tsc --noEmit`), understand what exists
3. Plan and implement improvements. Priority order:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - XR controller polish (ensure end-to-end controller support works across all game states — title screen, playing, aiming, menus, scorecard. Felix's #1 quality bar)
   - Visual polish: better course environments, hole-specific theming, more particle effects
   - Gameplay: ball spin/curve mechanics, stroke limit per hole, practice mode, multiplayer-ready scoring
   - Audio: background music per course, ambient environmental sounds, celebration jingles
   - UI: mini-map of current hole, stroke history replay, course flyover preview
   - Performance optimization
   - README with screenshots/GIF and deployment instructions
4. Push commits at milestones and redeploy to gh-pages using this pattern:
   ```
   cd ~/workspace/holo-golf
   git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
   npm run build
   PROJECT="$PWD"
   cd /tmp && rm -rf gh-pages-deploy && mkdir gh-pages-deploy && cd gh-pages-deploy
   git init && cp -R "$PROJECT/dist/." .
   git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
   git push --force "https://github.com/ellyz2426/holo-golf.git" HEAD:gh-pages
   ```
   GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope — use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.
5. When done, update `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status-pm.json`:
   - Append round 3 to `rounds` array with timing/stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 360 min total, or `"complete"` if ≥360
   - Set `last_updated_utc`
6. Capture your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo — commit and push
7. Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary-pm.md`

**Key quality bars (from Felix):**
- XR controller support is CRITICAL — trigger to fire, pointer for aiming, grip for power-ups, controller-based menu interaction
- Use IWSDK ECS CLI tools (ecs pause, step, snapshot) to verify fixes at runtime, not just code analysis
- Holodeck aesthetic: clean, futuristic, luminous, spatially legible
- Journal folder convention: `.agent/journal/` (NOT `.hatch/journal/`)
- Game repo: https://github.com/ellyz2426/holo-golf | Live: https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/"}

Agents are initializing and have not yet produced any results. Results will be delivered automatically when complete. Do not poll or sleep. Do not mention agent UUIDs to the user unless asked."}

item.text: Build agent dispatched for Holo Golf VR round 3. The game has 27 holes across 3 courses with 95 minutes of build time so far — the continuation will focus on XR controller polish, visual/gameplay enhancements, and audio improvements.

## Subagent Progress

<handoff>
All clean. Here's the summary:

---

## Round 3 Complete — Holo Golf VR

**Session summary:** 24 files, 6,185 lines (+1,029 from round 2). 135 of 360 target minutes used.

### What was accomplished:

**1. XR Controller Overhaul (Felix's #1 priority — DONE)**
- Complete rewrite of `xrinput.ts` — every button mapped for every game state
- Trigger → putt/select, A → confirm/skip, B → reset/back, Grip → power boost
- Left thumbstick → camera orbit (synced to browser camera system)
- Right thumbstick → menu navigation with focus highlight (outline + glow)
- Laser pointer visualization for menus and aiming
- VR control hints shown on all screens

**2. Course-Specific Environment Theming**
- 3 distinct visual themes: fog, grid, starfield, ambient geometry, point lights
- 3 distinct ambient music drones (different base freq, LFO rate, pad type)
- Dynamic theme switching on course start via `applyTheme()`

**3. Gameplay**
- 10-stroke limit per hole with HUD warning (color changes to red)
- Skip-to-next-hole via Space/Enter or VR A/Trigger
- Practice mode: replay individual holes with separate best scores

**4. Visual/Audio**
- Enhanced particle effects with course-specific palettes
- Pulsing celebration orbs for under-par and hole-in-one
- Stroke limit "wah-wah" descending SFX
- Memory cleanup: dispose geometry/materials on effect end

**5. UI**
- Mini-map overlay showing real-time hole layout, ball/tee/cup markers
- Mini result table in course complete screen
- Course-themed scorecard and HUD
- Updated controls help with full VR controller guide
- Comprehensive README

### Deployments
- 3 commits pushed to `master`, 2 deploys to `gh-pages`
- Live at: https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/
- All status files updated: `current-build-status-pm.json`, `BUILD_LOG.md`, `latest-build-summary-pm.md`
- Journal captured at `.agent/journal/2026-05-21-round3-pm.md`
</handoff>

parts.text:
You are a cron worker, not the main agent. Any assistant text you produced during execution is internal scratch text and has not been delivered to the user.

Your execution pass for this scheduled task run is complete.

Your task is to call exactly one final tool.

Before choosing, determine whether this run qualifies for silent completion.

Call `nothing_to_report({})` only if all of the following are true:
- There is no substantive result, alert, update, requested delivery, or execution-health issue to hand back to the main agent.
- The run had sufficient, fresh evidence for the task's requested time horizon.
- Every required dependency (API, data source, file, credential, tool, artifact, etc.) was fully available, current, and not replaced by a lower-quality workaround. There were no failures, gaps, stale inputs, partial checks, or inconsistent evidence.
- Silence accurately represents a clean, uneventful, fully successful run.

If not, or if you are unsure, call `notify_main_agent(message)` with a concise explanation. This is only an internal handoff; the main agent decides whether anything should surface externally.

These tools are terminal. Call exactly one of them, only once, and only now at the end of the run.
Do not continue executing the task. Do not call any other tool. Do not write a normal assistant reply instead of choosing one of the tools.
The main agent owns any later delivery and dedupe decision, including any external channel delivery.

[SCHEDULED TASK CONTEXT]
Read and follow the protocol at `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/checkin-protocol-pm.md`. This is the 2:00 PM check-in.

item.name: notify_main_agent
item.arguments:
{"message":"**Holo Golf VR — PM Build Round 3 complete** (2:00 PM check-in)

24 files, 6,185 lines (+1,029). 135 of 360 target minutes used. Status: `waiting_continuation`.

**Key additions this round:**
- **XR Controller overhaul** — complete rewrite of `xrinput.ts`: every button mapped for every game state (trigger→putt/select, A→confirm/skip, B→reset/back, grip→power boost, thumbsticks→camera orbit + menu nav with focus highlight, laser pointer for menus/aiming, VR control hints on all screens)
- **Course-specific environment theming** — 3 distinct visual themes (fog, grid, starfield, ambient geometry, point lights) + 3 ambient music drones, dynamic switching via `applyTheme()`
- **Gameplay** — 10-stroke limit per hole with HUD warning, skip-to-next-hole, practice mode with separate best scores
- **Visual/Audio** — enhanced particles with course-specific palettes, celebration orbs, stroke limit SFX, memory cleanup on effect end
- **UI** — mini-map overlay (real-time hole layout, ball/tee/cup markers), course-themed scorecard/HUD, full VR controller guide, comprehensive README

3 commits to `master`, 2 deploys to gh-pages. Live: https://ellyz2426.github.io/holo-golf/
Journal captured at `.agent/journal/2026-05-21-round3-pm.md`. Status files + BUILD_LOG.md updated."}
